rationale: The single ground-truth inline concern (whether the test should verify the new reduce_scatter attribute) is already resolved in the visible diff, so recall potential is thin for both; X at least explicitly validates that exact test region ('reduce_scatter only when passed; both branches reachable') while Y never touches it. X also produces five concrete, line-anchored, grounded findings (dtype mismatch vs upstream forward_context.py, missing cfg_parallel_size=1 test case, stale comment, missing docstring) each with file:line and cited evidence, giving it much higher actionability. Y is well-organized and its correctness verification is sound, but it surfaces only one soft, non-line-anchored suggestion and defaults to a blanket APPROVE despite admitting it couldn't execute anything, making it comparatively less useful as an actionable review.
